organization.organization_background: Utkrusht is a proof-of-skills marketplace that helps agile teams find talent with strong fundamental concepts with within days. We help aspiring professionals improve their profile with continuous improvement in their skills, documenting their journey and helping them fulfill their potential. We primarily build, conduct, proctor and disseminate proof-of-skill assessments in Technical domains like AI, ML, Fullstack development, High scale distributed systems, DevOps etc but do help non-tech roles like Sales, HR, Marketing etc

role_context: A BASIC-level Java-Spring MVC software engineer will independently implement small-to-medium web modules within a monolithic architecture, following a controller-service-repository pattern and delivering deployable artefacts (WAR or fat JAR). They design and expose straightforward web endpoints using @RequestMapping/@GetMapping/@PostMapping, bind inputs via @ModelAttribute and @Valid, and return appropriate views or JSON responses, while using simple validation and error handling. They configure Spring IoC with XML or @Configuration, manage common bean scopes, and implement transactional service methods with basic persistence through Spring JDBC Template or Spring Data JPA, including simple schema changes with scripts. They work with build tools (Maven/Gradle), operate in an IDE (IntelliJ/Eclipse/VS Code), write unit tests (JUnit 5) with basic mocks, and troubleshoot common issues with logs and basic profiling, all with limited supervision and in collaboration on story estimation and code reviews.
questions_prompt:
Please ensure the questions you ask cover
- - **Core Java & OOP Principles**: demonstrate solid Java SE coding practices using OOP concepts, generics, streams, lambdas, try-with-resources, and standard libraries; show understanding of exception handling, and basic memory/JVM concepts relevant to a small-to-medium Spring MVC module.
- - **Spring MVC Fundamentals & Request Flow**: demonstrate the ability to design controllers with @Controller, @RequestMapping/@GetMapping/@PostMapping, bind path vars, query params, headers, cookies, and multipart data; utilize @ModelAttribute, @Valid with JSR-303/380 validators, and return appropriate models and views following the PRG pattern; understand handler mapping, adapter, and view resolution.
- - **Persistence, Transactions & Data Access**: demonstrate integration with persistence via Spring JDBC Template or Spring Data JPA, configure a DataSource, apply @Transactional on service methods, perform basic CRUD, and map entities to DTOs; mention simple schema evolution strategies (e.g., Flyway) as applicable.
- - **Configuration, Packaging & Build**: demonstrate configuring Spring IoC via XML or @Configuration, use component stereotypes and qualifiers, manage bean scopes, externalize configuration with properties/YAML and profiles, and show how to package as WAR or run as a bootable fat JAR with builds (Maven/Gradle).
- - **Testing, Debugging & Quality**: demonstrate unit/integration testing with JUnit 5 and Mockito, test controllers with MockMvc, exercise basic QA tooling and code-quality practices (SpotBugs/PMD/Checkstyle/JaCoCo), and outline basic debugging/logging approaches (logs, Postman/curl) to diagnose common issues.

The goal is to evaluate...
